name: rasa.domain.canvas
description: The vertical-builder domain: authors and maintains canvas-vertical UIs (rasa.layout.v1 documents + sandboxed html artifacts) conversationally. Each app it manages is a session keyed by its own workspace directory; the domain bootstraps that directory, owns the app's canvas, handles ui_event interactions as the app's runtime brain, and keeps the screens honest to the component contract in content/.

rasa.tenant_model: The element definition is tenant-agnostic (the species); its INSTANCES are tenant-resident: sessions run with cwd inside a tenant's tree, so the tenant is the parent context (its CLAUDE.md/files/data in scope, kernel stamps tenant identity) and every app it manages is one of that tenant's UI surfaces. Composition target: tenant (where) x domain.* (about) x domain.canvas (how).
rasa.session_model: one app = one session, keyed (element=rasa.domain.canvas, cwd=<tenant>/.rasaos/apps/<app-id>). The app directory is bootstrapped by this domain on first contact and doubles as the app's persistent store, inside the tenant's own tree.
rasa.app_model: files are truth, the canvas is a projection. content/APP_MODEL.md defines the app directory exactly (app.json manifest with exhaustive screens[] + events[] registries, screens/ one layout file per screen, state/, per-app CHANGELOG) plus the write-order law (screen file -> app.json -> canvas_set) and three-clock versioning; content/PROCESSES.md defines the named procedures every turn runs (BOOTSTRAP, BUILD, EVENT, SWITCH_SCREEN, ADD_SCREEN, REBUILD, RETIRE). Enforcement: schemas/rasa.app.v1.schema.json publishes the manifest contract, bin/check-app audits an app directory and gates every publish (PROCESSES.md gate), bin/check-doctrine keeps the doctrine files in lockstep and gates every commit.
rasa.canvas_model: one canvas per app session (kernel keys canvases by session until args.canvas_id lands — see kernel asks in content/KERNEL_ASKS.md). The app may own MANY screens as files; exactly one (app.json#active_screen) is projected onto the canvas, siblings reachable via the button-row nav contract (nav:<screen-id>). The file model maps 1:1 onto named canvases when args.canvas_id ships.
rasa.note: The renderer contract lives with the shell (frontend-rasaos, SA-026/027/028); this element carries the AUTHORING doctrine. Keep the two in lockstep via content/COMPONENTS.md.

identity.one_liner: the RasaOS domain for building canvas-vertical UIs
identity.subject: canvas-vertical UI building
identity.goal: bring canvas-vertical UI building into a project as an installable RasaOS domain

files.note: Skill scaffold (opt-in). The generic /sync + /promote below install (directory-mirror); other skills stay opt-in.
Generic /sync — smart-pull upstream Element changes via the kit/<element>/ clone (canon SA-024).
Generic /promote — smart-push local changes back upstream via the kit/<element>/ clone (canon SA-024).

The DEPLOYMENT identity layer (SA-025) — project-owned; what this project is, for whom, on what platform. bin/init generates the element layer (.claude/rasa-identity.md) from rasa.identity every install.

scaffold.description: No scaffold directories — this domain operates in-session; its install surface is element.files[] + seed.files[] (identity layer + sync/promote skills).
